@@ -0,0 +1,117 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+depends_on:
+- 01KQ7GWE9V2XKWYAQ0HCPDE0EZ
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: aa80
+project: spatial-nav
+title: 'Fix: nav.up / nav.down from a focused card drops to no focus — direction-asymmetric registry bug'
+---
+## What
+
+After the cross-column fix lands (`01KQ7GWE9V2XKWYAQ0HCPDE0EZ`), pressing left or right from a focused card moves between columns correctly. **But pressing up or down from a focused card drops focus to nothing** — no card in the same column gets the focus indicator. Side-to-side works, vertical does not.
+
+The keymap is symmetric: `nav.up = k/ArrowUp`, `nav.down = j/ArrowDown`, `nav.left = h/ArrowLeft`, `nav.right = l/ArrowRight` — all four pass through the same `buildNavCommands` factory in `kanban-app/ui/src/components/app-shell.tsx` (around line 220-260) and call `spatial_navigate(focusedKey, direction)` with the same shape, only differing in the direction string. There is no `navOverride` configured at any call site (`grep -rn navOverride kanban-app/ui/src/` returns only primitive declarations, no usage). So the asymmetry must live in **what's focused** or **what gets registered for vertical-axis candidate selection**.
+
+### Suspect root causes (audit each, the test below pinpoints which)
+
+1. **Field zones are stealing focus from card leaves on click.** `<EntityCard>` wraps in `<FocusScope moniker="task:<id>">` (a leaf). Inside, `<Field>` renders a `<FocusZone moniker="field:task:<id>.<name>">`. When the user clicks on a card, the click might land on the title text (inside the Field zone) rather than on the card body whitespace. The Field zone's `onClick` calls `spatial_focus(field-zone-key)` and stops propagation — so the card scope never receives the click. The focused entry is a Field **zone**, not a card leaf.
+
+   When focused on a zone, the kernel runs `navigate_zone` (`swissarmyhammer-focus/src/navigate.rs`), which restricts candidates to **sibling zones only with no rule-2 leaf fallback**. A Field's sibling zones are other Field zones in the same `parent_zone`. Up/down from `field:task:T1.title` → finds `field:task:T1.status` (works for one or two presses), then nothing below the last field of T1 (no rule-2 fallback to `task:T2`'s fields, since the kernel has no zone-level rule 2). Returns `None`. Focus stays on the field zone, whose indicator is rendered inline and may look like nothing.
+
+   This also explains why left/right "works": from a Field zone, the next column's same-row content is *also* a Field zone (Fields registered with `parent_zone = column`, so siblings span columns? actually NO — siblings means same parent_zone, which means same column). Re-examine — if Field zones have `parent_zone = column`, they're not siblings across columns. So either the parent_zone IS something more permissive, OR the user perception "side to side works" needs verification (the focus might be moving to a Field in the next column via the Field-zone graph, not to a card).
+
+2. **Field zones register with `parent_zone = ui:board` instead of `parent_zone = column`.** The card's wrapper `<FocusScope>` is a leaf and does NOT establish a `<FocusZoneContext>` provider — so a Field rendered inside the card reads `useParentZoneKey()` and gets the *next outer* zone, which is the column. That's correct. But **if the Field itself has a `<FocusZone>` provider that the card's children re-read**, the parent_zone might collapse upward. Verify in the test by capturing every `spatial_register_zone` payload and printing the `parent_zone` for every `field:*` entry.
+
+3. **Cards register with stale rects on initial layout.** If the card's initial rect at registration time is `{0,0,0,0}` (registered before layout), then later `ResizeObserver` updates it — but the kernel might have a quirk where Down's early-return (`major < 0 && cand.bottom <= from.bottom`) treats zero-height rects asymmetrically across direction. Unlikely root cause but cheap to verify.
+
+4. **An unrelated focusable inside the column has a wider horizontal extent that wins rule 1 for Down.** E.g. an "Add Task" button at the bottom, a ghost drop-zone, a virtualizer placeholder. From `task:T1`, Down's rule 1 finds the chrome focusable below T1 (in-beam, smaller major than T2) and routes there. The chrome focusable has no visible indicator. This was suspect 6 in the cross-column fix card (`01KQ7GWE9V2XKWYAQ0HCPDE0EZ`); if that card's fix didn't audit it, it's still latent for the vertical axis.
+
+### Files to audit and likely fix
+
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/field.tsx` — confirm what `parent_zone` Field zones actually register with; consider whether Field zones should be Field **leaves** (and let the editor own its own internal nav) or whether the card body should itself be a `<FocusZone>` so Fields parent under the card. The card-as-zone option was rejected earlier (would break cross-column rule 2), so Field-as-leaf is the more likely fix.
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/entity-card.tsx` — confirm the card's `onClick` actually focuses the card (not the inner Field zone) when the user clicks on the card body.
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/column-view.tsx` — confirm no chrome focusable (Add Task button, drop zone, virtualizer placeholder) is registering as a leaf with `parent_zone = column` and a horizontally-wide rect.
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-scope.tsx` and `focus-zone.tsx` — read-only — confirm click handlers' `e.stopPropagation()` semantics behave as expected when a Field zone lives inside a card scope.
+
+### What this task does NOT do
+
+- Does not change the Rust beam-search algorithm. The kernel is correct; the bug is in registry shape or focus-target resolution.
+- Does not introduce a `navOverride` to paper over the bug.
+- Does not touch the keymap dispatch — that's symmetric and correct.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- [ ] A browser test focuses `task:T1` (a card in column TODO) and presses `j` (vim) / `ArrowDown` (cua). Asserts the resulting `data-focused="true"` element is `task:T2` (the next card in column TODO), NOT a Field zone, NOT a chrome focusable, NOT undefined.
+- [ ] Same test for `nav.up` from `task:T2` lands on `task:T1`.
+- [ ] Same test for `nav.up` from the topmost card in a column lands on the column header leaf (`column.name`) — the existing rule-1 in-zone behavior.
+- [ ] Cross-column right/left from a card still moves to a card in the next column (regression guard for `01KQ7GWE9V2XKWYAQ0HCPDE0EZ`).
+- [ ] **Registry shape diagnostic:** the test captures every `spatial_register_*` invoke and prints, on failure, a tree of zones / leaves with their `parent_zone`. The diagnostic is part of the test's normal assertion failure output — the implementer must use it to identify which root cause is real before writing the fix, then keep the diagnostic permanently so the next regression is easy to debug.
+- [ ] The fix introduces no `navOverride` at any call site.
+- [ ] `cd kanban-app/ui && npm test` is green.
+- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-focus -p swissarmyhammer-kanban` is green (no kernel regressions).
+
+## Tests
+
+### Browser Tests (mandatory)
+
+Run under Vitest browser mode (`vitest-browser-react` + Playwright Chromium) using the same shadow-registry + ported `BeamNavStrategy` harness as `kanban-app/ui/src/components/board-view.cross-column-nav.spatial.test.tsx`. The harness is being extracted to a shared helper (`kanban-app/ui/src/test/spatial-shadow-registry.ts`) under task `01KQ7PXYP62VQ18K9XYS4Y42GA` — if that helper exists when this card starts, import from it; otherwise inline the harness here and refactor to import from the helper as a follow-up.
+
+#### Test file
+`kanban-app/ui/src/components/board-view.vertical-card-nav.spatial.test.tsx` (new file)
+
+#### Setup
+
+- Mock `@tauri-apps/api/core` and `@tauri-apps/api/event` per the canonical pattern.
+- Build the shadow registry from captured `spatial_register_*` calls. Stub `spatial_navigate(key, direction)` with the ported `BeamNavStrategy` so the kernel algorithm runs against the real production wiring's registrations.
+- Render the full board view inside the standard provider stack — `<UIStateProvider><PerspectivesProvider><ViewsProvider><BoardDataProvider><SpatialFocusProvider><FocusLayer name="main"><BoardView/></FocusLayer></SpatialFocusProvider>…</UIStateProvider>` — same fixture as the cross-column test (board with 2-3 columns × 3 cards).
+- Capture the registered keys for `task:T1`, `task:T2`, `task:T3`, `column.name`, the column zones, and any Field zones / chrome focusables that exist after mount.
+
+#### Required test cases
+
+1. **Down from T1 lands on T2** — `fireFocusChanged(task:T1)`. Press `ArrowDown`. Assert `data-focused="true"` lands on `[data-moniker="task:T2"]` after `fireFocusChanged` is emitted by the harness's ported navigator with the expected next-key.
+
+2. **Up from T2 lands on T1** — mirror.
+
+3. **Down from T3 (last card in column)** — runs rule-1 (no card below), runs rule-2 (cross-zone leaf fallback): if there's a card in another column whose top is below T3.bottom, that card wins. Otherwise (typical kanban layout — all columns same height), rule 2 finds no candidate and Down is genuinely a no-op. The test asserts the no-op case explicitly: focus stays on T3, no `focus-changed` event, no `data-focused` flicker. Document this as the spec — vertical edge of the column is NOT a fall-through to another column's top.
+
+4. **Up from T1 lands on column header leaf** — column.name is above T1, in-beam horizontally. Rule 1 finds it. `data-focused="true"` lands on `[data-moniker$=".name"]`.
+
+5. **Click on a card title (Field-zone child) → press Down** — this is the diagnostic case for suspect 1. Click on the card's title text element (inside the Field zone). Read `mockInvoke` calls: was `spatial_focus` called with the card's key, the Field zone's key, or both? Then press Down. Whichever moniker is focused, the test asserts the next focused moniker is reasonable: if focus landed on the card → next is `task:T2`; if focus landed on the Field zone → next is the next-row Field zone OR the test fails with a clear message that "clicking on a Field steals focus from the card and Field-zone vertical nav has no rule-2 fallback." This case's failure mode pinpoints whether the bug is "focus steal" or "rule-2 missing for zones" or both.
+
+6. **Registry shape diagnostic** — print on failure (and assert as a positive check):
+   - All `task:*` registered as `spatial_register_focusable` (leaves), never as `spatial_register_zone`.
+   - All `field:*` registered as `spatial_register_zone`, with `parent_zone === <column zone key>` (NOT `ui:board`'s key, NOT a card key, NOT null).
+   - All `column:*` registered with `parent_zone === <ui:board zone key>`.
+   - Single `layer_key` across all registrations.
+   - List every leaf with `parent_zone === <column zone key>`. The expected set is exactly: `column.name`, `task:T1`, `task:T2`, `task:T3`. Any extra leaf (chrome button, drop zone) is a smoking gun for suspect 4.
+
+7. **Cross-column right still works** — regression guard. From `task:T1`, press ArrowRight. Lands on a card in column DOING.
+
+#### Backend test (kernel regression guard)
+
+- [ ] Add a Rust integration test in `swissarmyhammer-focus/tests/navigate.rs` named `rule_1_realistic_column_down_from_card_lands_on_next_card`. Builds the same realistic shape: a `ui:board` zone, one `column:TODO` zone with a `column.name` leaf and three `task:*` leaves stacked vertically. Plus three `field:*` zones per card with their `parent_zone` set to the column. Asserts `nav("task:T1", Direction::Down) == Some(Moniker("task:T2"))` and `nav("task:T2", Direction::Up) == Some(Moniker("task:T1"))`.
+
+  This guards against a future kernel change that ignores in-zone leaves when sibling zones exist at the same parent level — the current implementation correctly filters leaves and zones by their tagged kind, but the kernel should also have a regression test for it.
+
+  Run: `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-focus`.
+
+### How to run
+
+```
+cd kanban-app/ui && npm test
+cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-focus -p swissarmyhammer-kanban
+```
+
+Both must pass headless.
+
+## Workflow
+
+- Use `/tdd`. Order:
+  1. Write the failing test (case 1: Down from T1 → T2). Run it. The failure mode is the diagnosis: read which moniker actually got `data-focused="true"`, what `spatial_navigate` returned, what the registered registry shape was. Decide which suspect (1 / 2 / 3 / 4) is real.
+  2. Apply the fix (most likely changing Field's wrap to a leaf or fixing Field's `parent_zone`, OR removing a chrome focusable from the column body, OR fixing the click → spatial_focus path so card-body clicks don't steal-focus to a Field zone).
+  3. Confirm cases 1-7 pass.
+  4. Add the kernel regression test.
+
+This card may be closed-by-subsumption when `01KQ7PXYP62VQ18K9XYS4Y42GA` (end-to-end smoke test) lands, since Family 2 of that test is exactly this case. But ship the fix on this card first — the end-to-end card depends on this one's fix landing, and the targeted browser test here is the right place for the diagnostic-rich failure mode.

+### Suspect root causes (audit each, the test below pinpoints which)
+
+1. **Field zones are stealing focus from card leaves on click.** `<EntityCard>` wraps in `<FocusScope moniker="task:<id>">` (a leaf). Inside, `<Field>` renders a `<FocusZone moniker="field:task:<id>.<name>">`. When the user clicks on a card, the click might land on the title text (inside the Field zone) rather than on the card body whitespace. The Field zone's `onClick` calls `spatial_focus(field-zone-key)` and stops propagation — so the card scope never receives the click. The focused entry is a Field **zone**, not a card leaf.
+
+   When focused on a zone, the kernel runs `navigate_zone` (`swissarmyhammer-focus/src/navigate.rs`), which restricts candidates to **sibling zones only with no rule-2 leaf fallback**. A Field's sibling zones are other Field zones in the same `parent_zone`. Up/down from `field:task:T1.title` → finds `field:task:T1.status` (works for one or two presses), then nothing below the last field of T1 (no rule-2 fallback to `task:T2`'s fields, since the kernel has no zone-level rule 2). Returns `None`. Focus stays on the field zone, whose indicator is rendered inline and may look like nothing.
+
+   This also explains why left/right "works": from a Field zone, the next column's same-row content is *also* a Field zone (Fields registered with `parent_zone = column`, so siblings span columns? actually NO — siblings means same parent_zone, which means same column). Re-examine — if Field zones have `parent_zone = column`, they're not siblings across columns. So either the parent_zone IS something more permissive, OR the user perception "side to side works" needs verification (the focus might be moving to a Field in the next column via the Field-zone graph, not to a card).
+
+2. **Field zones register with `parent_zone = ui:board` instead of `parent_zone = column`.** The card's wrapper `<FocusScope>` is a leaf and does NOT establish a `<FocusZoneContext>` provider — so a Field rendered inside the card reads `useParentZoneKey()` and gets the *next outer* zone, which is the column. That's correct. But **if the Field itself has a `<FocusZone>` provider that the card's children re-read**, the parent_zone might collapse upward.
